messages.content:
You are Research Desk, a helpful research assistant.

# Research Desk Rules

## Citations
- Include source URLs inline: [title](url)
- For papers: cite as [title](https://arxiv.org/abs/{arxiv_id})
- Prefer primary sources (papers, official docs) over blog posts

## Papers (required tools)
- Use `paper_search` for ML research and literature questions
- Use `read_paper` with the arxiv_id from search results â€” do not guess IDs
- If `read_paper` returns 404, fall back to `web_fetch` on arxiv.org/abs/...
- Do not use web_search when paper_search is the right tool

## Research notes
- Save new content with `write_file` to `notes/`
- Update existing notes with `read_file` then `edit_file` â€” do not rewrite whole files unnecessarily
- Use `edit_file` operations: `append` for new sections, `replace` to revise, `delete` to remove stale parts
- Keep edits inside `notes/` unless the user explicitly asks otherwise
- Use lowercase hyphenated filenames: `notes/topic-name.md`

## Web search
- Use `web_search` before `web_fetch` for non-paper questions
- Do not fetch more than 3 pages per question unless the user asks for depth

## Tone
- Be concise in chat; put detail in the note files
Who is the best wicket keeper batsman in the history of Indian cricket??

The title of “best wicket‑keeper‑batsman in Indian cricket history” is widely credited to **Mahendra Singh Dhoni**.  

**Why Dhoni stands out**

| Aspect | Dhoni’s Record | Why It matters |
|--------|---------------|----------------|
| **Test keeping** | 2,273 dismissals (173 catches + 43 stumpings) – the most by any Indian keeper in Tests | Shows consistent high‑level glove work over a long career |
| **ODI batting** | 10,773 runs at 50.57 average, 10 centuries, 73 fifties; 4 × Player of the Match in World Cups | Combines run‑scoring reliability with the ability to finish games |
| **T20I batting** | 1,889 runs at 37.78, 2 centuries (first keeper to score a T20I hundred) | Demonstrates adaptability to the shortest format |
| **Captaincy** | Led India to 2007 ICC T20 World Cup, 2011 ICC Cricket World Cup, 2013 ICC Champions Trophy (only captain to win all three major ICC trophies) | Adds leadership value to his keeper‑batsman role |
| **Impact in clutch moments** | “Finisher” reputation – 91 runs off 58 balls vs Sri Lanka (2011 WC semi‑final) and 91* off 79 vs Pakistan (2012 Asia T20) | Proven ability to win matches under pressure |
| **Longevity** | International career 2004–2019 (15 years) | Sustained performance at the highest level |

**Comparison with other Indian greats**

| Player | Runs (ODI) | Avg (ODI) | Dismissals (Tests) | Notable achievements |
|--------|-----------|----------|--------------------|----------------------|
| **Syed Ali Muzammil “MS Dhoni”** – see above | 10,773 | 50.57 | 2,273 | 2× World Cup winner, 1× T20 World Cup winner |
| **Kumar Sangakkara** (Sri Lanka, but Indian‑born) – not Indian | — | — | — | — |
| **Wriddhiman Saha** | 2,347 | 31.57 | 254 | Excellent technique, limited by injuries |
| **Rishabh Pant** (active) | 2,638 | 31.59 | 219 | Aggressive batting, still early in career |
| **Parthiv Patel** | 1,932 | 30.49 | 85 | Early debut, solid keeper but lower batting stats |

Statistically and historically, Dhoni’s combination of high batting average, massive run tally, record‑setting dismissals, and unparalleled captaincy success eclipses all other Indian wicket‑keeper‑batsmen.  

**Bottom line:** While players like **Wriddhiman Saha** and **Rishabh Pant** have shown great skill, **Mahendra S. Dhoni** remains the most accomplished and influential wicket‑keeper‑batsman in the annals of Indian cricket.
